english: Lt. id. — 2. vb., also p̊rág-pa (phrag pa), to envy, to grudge, Cs.; p̊ray-dóg (phrag dog), p̊ra-dóg (phra dog), the envy, p̊ray-dog (phrag dog) skye (skye) envy is stirring within me, I envy, frq.; p̊rag-dóg-can (phrag dog can), envious, grudging, jealous Pth.
tsig-p̊rád (tshig phrad), p̊rád-kyi (phrad kyi) yi-ge (yi ge), particle, e.g. rnám-dbye-p̊rad (rnam dbye phrad) the signs of the cases, kyi (kyi), la (la) etc.
v. p̊rád-pa ('phrad pa); p̊rád-po (phrad po) for krád-po (khrad po) Wdn.

(Ts. also p̊rán-te (phran te)) = p̊rá-mo (phra mo), little, small, trifling, yet more in particular phrases, and less used in books, than in common life, esp. in C.: *rin ṭeṁ-bhu ṭe-dhe* (lit.: sprad-de (sprad de)) having paid, spent a trifle; *żú-ba ṭeṁ-bhu żig* a small request; *ṭem-bhu ċig* a little bit C.; as sbst.: 1. part of the body (whether in a general or a more particular sense, I have not been able to ascertain); in medical writings the p̊ran-bui (phran bu'i) nad (nad) form a class of their own; yan-lág-gi (yan lag gi) p̊ran (phran) yćód-pa (gcod pa) Glr., to maim, to mutilate parts of the body (not necessarily to castrate Sch.). — 2. knives and other small instruments used in surgery Med. — 3. p̊ran-ráṅ (phran rang) in the polite epistolary style the person of the writer, 'my own little self', 'your humble servant'; p̊rán-la (phran la) ráṅ-gi (rang gi) = to me my..., inst. of: ṅá-la (nga la) ṅa-ráṅ-gi (nga rang gi). — p̊ran-tségs (phran tshegs), trifles, minor matters; dúl-ba ('dul ba) p̊ran-tségs-kyi (phran tshegs kyi) gżi (gzhi) the minutiae of religious discipline, Dulva.
p̊ran-ne-rtsag-tsi (phran ne rtsag tsi) stated to be = pyin-ċi-lóg (phyin ci log) Ld.

news, tidings, intelligence, message, p̊rin (phrin) bzáṅ-po (bzang po), good tidings, favourable accounts; p̊rin-bkur-mkan (phrin bkur mkhan), messenger, vulgo; p̊rin (phrin) skúr-ba (skur ba), spríṅ-ba (spring ba) to send word, information, kyér-ba (khyer ba), to bring tidings, intelligence; spród-pa (sprod pa), p̊ród-pa ('phrod pa) to deliver; smrá-ba (smra ba), rjód-pa (rjod pa), byéd-pa (byed pa) to report, to deliver messages orally; to superiors: gsól-ba (gsol ba), żú-ba (zhu ba); to inferiors: sgó-ba (sgo ba), gsúṅ-ba (gsung ba); kỏ-boi (kho bo'i) p̊rin (phrin) yaṅ (yang) dé-la (de la) byós (byos) śig (shig) deliver a message to him also from me Dzl.; p̊rin-ytam (phrin gtam) message, report Cs.; p̊rin-pa (phrin pa) messenger; news-monger Cs.; p̊rin-bzáṅ (phrin bzang) gospel Chr. Prot.; p̊rin-yíg (phrin yig) letter, epistle; p̊rin-lán (phrin lan) answer to a message. — p̊rin-lás (phrin las) (W. *čag-lás*) 1. resp. for las (las) labour, business; deed, work, frq.; ráb-tu-gnás-pai (rab tu gnas pa'i) p̊rin-las (phrin las) mdzad (mdzad) (the Buddhas) performed the work of consecrating Glr.; p̊rin-lás (phrin las) rnam (rnam) bżi (bzhi) the same as żi-rgyas-dbaṅ-drág-gi (zhi rgyas dbang drag gi) p̊rin-lás (phrin las) Glr., v. explanation under żi-ba (zhi ba); p̊rin-lás (phrin las) ćól-ba (bcol ba), p̊rin-bcól (phrin bcol) byéd-pa (byed pa) ccdpar. to commit a thing to another person's care or trust, e.g. before going on a journey; in reference to gods: to recommend to their protection or blessing Glr. and elsewh. — 2. po. for p̊rin-lás-pa (phrin las pa) commissary Glr., where Avalokitesvara is called p̊rin-lás (phrin las) of all Buddhas. — 3. efficiency, power Mil.
, 'p̊rú-ba ('phru ba) = kóg-ma (khog ma) earthen pot, pan, stew-pan.
, 'p̊rú-ma ('phru ma) 1. uterus, matrix of animals, or acc. to Cs. merely the integuments of the eggs; acc. to some, also the urinary bladder. — 2. encampment, = dmag-sgár (dmag sgar) Lex.

1. in compounds for p̊rúg-gu (phrug gu), p̊rú-gu (phru gu) child, a young one (of animals); p̊rúg-gu-mo (phrug gu mo) a little girl Cs.; p̊rúg-gu (phrug gu) skyéd-pa (skyed pa) to beget children, gsó-ba (gso ba) to rear, to bring up (children); p̊rúg-gu (phrug gu) skye (skye) a child is born; śor (shor) a miscarriage, abortion, takes place; p̊rúg-gui (phrug gu'i) dus (dus) childhood; dá-p̊rug (da phrug) orphan; nal-p̊rug (nal phrug) bastard; gláṅ-p̊rug (glang phrug) the young one of an elephant; séṅ-p̊rug (seng phrug) a lion's cub etc.; metaph. of disciples and subalterns: tsoṅ-p̊rúg (tshong phrug) the merchants of a caravan in their relationship to their leader tsoṅ-dpón (tshong dpon). — 2. fine cloth or woollen stuffs Wts., snam-p̊rúg (snam phrug) id., dbus-p̊rúg (dbus phrug) woollen goods from Ü Mil.
one day with the night, a period of 24 hours, — but this signification does not hold good in every case. <
